title: Prohibited AI practices
text: Prohibited: social scoring by public authorities producing detrimental treatment; untargeted scraping of facial images from internet/CCTV for FR databases; real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement (narrow exceptions); emotion recognition in workplace + education; categorization based on sensitive characteristics. Penalty up to €35M / 7% global turnover.

title: High-risk AI use cases
text: Biometrics; critical infrastructure; education/vocational training (admission, grading); employment + workers management (recruitment, evaluation, dismissal); access to essential services (credit scoring, benefits, emergency dispatch, insurance); law enforcement; migration/asylum/border control; administration of justice + democratic processes.

title: Risk management system
text: Continuous, iterative risk management system across entire lifecycle: identification + analysis of known + foreseeable risks; estimation + evaluation; targeted measures to manage; testing for purpose of identifying most appropriate measures. Updated regularly.

title: Data governance + management
text: Training, validation, testing data subject to data governance + management practices: design choices, data collection, processing operations (annotation, labeling, cleaning, enrichment, aggregation), assumptions, examination for biases + appropriate measures, gaps + shortcomings. Bias representativeness statement required.

section: EU AI Act Art 11 + Annex IV
title: Technical documentation
text: Annex IV required: general description (intended purpose, version, providers), detailed description (architecture, training data, model card), monitoring + control, risk management, performance metrics, post-market monitoring plan, EU declaration of conformity. Maintained throughout lifecycle + 10 years post-market.

title: Record-keeping (logs)
text: High-risk AI must automatically record events ('logs') over the lifetime appropriate to intended purpose. Logs retained ≥6 months unless other EU/national law requires longer. Logs must allow traceability of system operation including for post-market monitoring.

title: Human oversight
text: High-risk AI designed + developed to enable effective human oversight by natural persons during use. Measures include: understand capacities + limitations; remain aware of automation bias; correctly interpret output; disregard/override/reverse output; stop the system. Person performing oversight must have necessary competence, training, authority.

title: Conformity assessment procedures
text: Module A (internal control, used for most Annex III high-risk systems based on standards) or Module H1 (full quality assurance with notified body for biometrics + cases without harmonised standards). Output: EU declaration of conformity + CE marking + registration in EU database (Art 71). Annex VII assessment procedure detailed.

title: GPAI provider obligations
text: General-purpose AI model providers: technical documentation, information to downstream providers integrating model, comply with EU copyright (training data text + data mining opt-out), publish summary of training content. Systemic risk GPAI (Art 51, e.g., FLOPs > 10^25): additional model evaluation + adversarial testing + serious incident reporting + cybersecurity protection.

title: Serious incident reporting
text: Providers of high-risk AI must report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities of MS where incident occurred. Timing: immediately after causal link established + at latest 15 days after awareness. Serious widespread/cascade event: 2 days. Death: 10 days. Investigation cooperation required.

title: AI Risk Management Framework
text: Four functions: Govern (policies, accountability, oversight); Map (context, risks, impacts); Measure (analysis, assessment, tracking); Manage (prioritize, treat, communicate). Three dimensions: trustworthy AI characteristics (valid + reliable, safe, secure + resilient, accountable + transparent, explainable + interpretable, privacy-enhanced, fair). Companion Playbook with detailed guidance.

title: AI Management System (AIMS)
text: International standard for AI management systems. Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle. Annex A controls (38 controls across 9 categories: AI policy, internal organisation, resources, impact assessment, AI lifecycle, data, info for interested parties, use of AI, third-party relationships). AI policy must be signed by top management.

title: Automated decisions affecting individuals
text: Data subject has right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing (including profiling) which produces legal effects or similarly significant effects. Exceptions: necessary for contract, authorised by Union/MS law, explicit consent. Even when exception applies, controller must implement suitable measures including the right to human intervention, express their point of view, contest the decision.

title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA)
text: Public-sector deployers + private deployers providing public services using Annex III high-risk AI must perform FRIA before first deployment. Contents: description of processes, time period + frequency, categories of affected individuals + groups, specific risks of harm, human oversight measures, mitigation in case of materialisation. Notification to market surveillance authority.
